growth_records.strain: Enrichment community (>50% C. kluyveri-related OTU by 16S rRNA), inoculated from anaerobic digester sludge (Harnaschpolder, Netherlands)
growth_records.medium: Adapted Clostridium kluyveri medium (acetate + ethanol, 6:1 EtOH:acetate)

up.method: unit conversion of 0.54 mol/Cmol_X/h using biomass MW 24.6 g/Cmol

sec.method: reported electron distribution (dominant product at pH 7.0)

yields.product_yields: EtOH:H2 = 3.0 +/- 0.3 mol/mol; donor:acceptor consumption ratio = 1.9 +/- 0.1 mol/mol (acceptor = acetate + butyrate consumed)

growth_records.cite: Allaart MT et al. Product Inhibition and pH Affect Stoichiometry and Kinetics of Chain Elongating Microbial Communities in Sequencing Batch Bioreactors. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2021). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2021.693030. [PMC8256265]
growth_records.snippet: At pH 7.0, the consumed acetate and ethanol were converted to mainly caproate, comprising 71 +/- 6% of the electrons found in fermentation products... Y_X/EtOH 0.11 +/- 0.02 Cmol_X mol-1; q_EtOH 0.54 +/- 0.08 mol Cmol_X-1 h-1; EtOH:H2 3.0 +
growth_records.notes: Steady (cycles 60-96) SBR enrichment at pH 7.0. Growth rate imposed by exchange ratio 0.5 and 12 h cycle: biomass doubles each cycle so td=12 h, mu=ln2/12=0.0578~0.058 h-1 (reported 0.058). Y_X/EtOH=0.11+/-0.02 Cmol_X/mol (Table 1). q_EtOH reported 0.54+/-0.08 mol/Cmol_X/h; paper defines q_EtOH=mu/Y_X/EtOH. Conversion to mmol/gDW/h uses stated biomass MW 24.6 g/Cmol: 0.54 mol/Cmol/h *1000 /24.6 = 21.95 mmol/gDW/h. Ethanol consumption 47+/-4%, acetate consumption 79+/-3%; acetate uptake derived from consumed-mmol ratio: (0.79*21.1)/(0.47*125.5)=0.283 -> 0.283*21.95=6.21 mmol/gDW/h. qH2 derived from EtOH:H2=3.0: 21.95/3.0=7.32 mmol/gDW/h. Electrons to caproate 71+/-6% (Table 1); remainder mainly butyrate. Donor:acceptor 1.9+/-0.1. Reported Y comparable to Candry et al. 2018 (0.11) and Kenealy & Waselefsky 1985 (0.07-0.13). Mixed community (not axenic); products caproate/butyrate/H2; CO2 co-consumed.

growth_records.strain: Enrichment community (>50% C. kluyveri-related OTU by 16S rRNA), inoculated from anaerobic digester sludge (Harnaschpolder, Netherlands)
growth_records.medium: Adapted Clostridium kluyveri medium (acetate + ethanol, 6:1 EtOH:acetate)

up.method: unit conversion of 1.12 mol/Cmol_X/h using biomass MW 24.6 g/Cmol

sec.method: reported: butyrate-dominated spectrum at pH 5.5; caproate only 30% of electrons

yields.product_yields: EtOH:H2 = 3.5 +/- 0.2 mol/mol; donor:acceptor consumption ratio = 2.1 +/- 0.2 mol/mol (acceptor = acetate + butyrate consumed)

growth_records.cite: Allaart MT et al. Product Inhibition and pH Affect Stoichiometry and Kinetics of Chain Elongating Microbial Communities in Sequencing Batch Bioreactors. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2021). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2021.693030. [PMC8256265]
growth_records.snippet: Operating at pH 5.5 led to a decrease in the biomass yield, but a significant increase in the biomass-specific substrate uptake rate... Y_X/EtOH 0.05 +/- 0.01 Cmol_X mol-1; q_EtOH 1.12 +/- 0.13 mol Cmol_X-1 h-1; electrons to caproate 30 +/-
growth_records.notes: Steady (cycles 60-96) SBR enrichment at pH 5.5. Same imposed growth rate mu=0.058 h-1 (td=12 h; biomass doubles per 12 h cycle at 50% exchange). Y_X/EtOH=0.05+/-0.01 Cmol_X/mol (Table 1); reduced vs pH 7.0 due to weak-acid uncoupling. q_EtOH reported 1.12+/-0.13 mol/Cmol_X/h = mu/Y = 0.058/0.05. Conversion using biomass MW 24.6 g/Cmol: 1.12*1000/24.6 = 45.53 mmol/gDW/h. Ethanol consumption 25+/-3%, acetate consumption 66+/-4%; acetate uptake derived: (0.66*21.1)/(0.25*125.5)=0.444 -> 0.444*45.53=20.21 mmol/gDW/h. qH2 from EtOH:H2=3.5: 45.53/3.5=13.01 mmol/gDW/h. Product spectrum butyrate-dominated (caproate only 30+/-5% of product electrons). Y at pH 5.5 close to Thauer et al. 1968 (0.06). Donor:acceptor 2.1+/-0.2, within range of C. kluyveri acetate-limited chemostats (1.7-2.3, Kenealy & Waselefsky 1985). Mixed community, not axenic.
